\(\dfrac{1}{x+1} + \dfrac{1}{x-1} = \)

A. \(\dfrac{1}{x+1}\)
B. \(\dfrac{1}{x-1}\)
C. \(\dfrac{2x}{x^2-1}\)
D. \(\dfrac{1}{(x+1)(x-1)}\)
E. \(\dfrac{x^2+2x+1}{(x+1)(x-1)}\)

If \(x = \dfrac{a+by}{c-by}\), then \(y = \)

A. \(\dfrac{cx-a}{b(x+1)}\)
B. \(\dfrac{cx+a}{b(x-1)}\)
C. \(\dfrac{cx-a}{b(x-1)}\)
D. \(\dfrac{cx+a}{b(x+1)}\)
E. \(\dfrac{ax-bx-c}{a-bx}\)

\(\dfrac{xy-1}{x} + \dfrac{y-x}{xy} = \)

A. \(\dfrac{x-y}{x+y}\)
B. \(\dfrac{x+y}{x-y}\)
C. \(\dfrac{x+y}{x-y}\)
D. \(\dfrac{x^2+2}{x^2}\)
E. \(\dfrac{x^2-y^2}{x^2}\)

If \(H = K + M\sqrt[n]{4(r^2+l^2)}\) and \(r \gt 0\), then \(r = \)

A. \(\sqrt{\left(\dfrac{H-K}{M}\right)^n \dfrac{1}{4} - l^2}\)
B. \(\sqrt{\left(\dfrac{H-K}{M}\right)^n \dfrac{1}{4} - l^2}\)
C. \(\sqrt{\left(\dfrac{H-K}{M}\right)^n \dfrac{1}{4} - l^2}\)
D. \(\sqrt{\left(\dfrac{H-K}{M}\right)^n \dfrac{1}{4} - l^2}\)
E. \(\sqrt{\left(\dfrac{H-K}{M}\right)^n \dfrac{1}{4} - l^2}\)

If \(f(x) = x^2+x+1\), then \(f(x+1)-f(x) = \)

A. \(1\)
B. \(3\)
C. \(2x+1\)
D. \(2x+2\)
E. \(x^2+x+1\)

If \(\log_{10}x + \log_{10}4 = \log_{10}(x+4)\), what is the value of \(x\)?

A. \(0\)
B. \(1\)
C. \(4/3\)
D. \(4\)
E. \(x\) maybe any positive number

A. \(0\) only
B. \(7\) only
C. \(0\) or \(7\)
D. \(3/4\) or \(2\)
E. \(0, 3/4\) or \(2\)

\(2y-3 \gt 4y+2x+5\) is equivalent to

A. \(y \gt x+4\)
B. \(y \lt x+4\)
C. \(y \gt -x-4\)
D. \(y \lt -x-4\)
E. \(y \gt x+1\)

The nth term of the arithmetic progression \(2, 6, 10, 14, \dots\) is

A. \(2n^2\)
B. \(4n\)
C. \(4n-2\)
D. \(4n+2\)
E. \(6-4n\)

The marked price of a book is \(\$x\). \(30\%\) of this price is profit. If the book is sold at a discount of \(20\%\), what will the profit then be?

A. \(\$0.04x\)
B. \(\$0.06x\)
C. \(\$0.1x\)
D. \(\$0.24x\)
E. \(\$0.56x\)

A group consists of \(n\) boys and \(n\) girls. If two of the girls are exceeded by two other boys, then \(51\%\) of the group members will be boys. What is \(n\)?

A. \(50\)
B. \(51\)
C. \(52\)
D. \(100\)
E. \(102\)

If the surface area of a spherical soap bubble increases by \(44\%\), its volume increases by

A. \(20\%\)
B. \(33.1\%\)
C. \(60\%\)
D. \(66\%\)
E. \(72.8\%\)

The total area of the six faces of the solid cube in the figure is \(96 \text{ cm}^2\). What is the length of the diagonal \(AB\)?

A. \(6\sqrt{2} \text{ cm}\)
B. \(4\sqrt{3} \text{ cm}\)
C. \(4\sqrt{2} \text{ cm}\)
D. \(2\sqrt{6} \text{ cm}\)
E. \(4 \text{ cm}\)

A merchant sold \(100\) chairs. \(80\) of them were sold at a profit of \(30\%\) on each chair, while \(20\) of them were sold at a loss of \(40\%\) on each chair. What is his percentage gain or loss on the whole stock?

A. A loss of \(8\%\)
B. A loss of \(10\%\)
C. A gain of \(8\%\)
D. A gain of \(16\%\)
E. A gain of \(24\%\)

A. \(0\)

A. \(x \le 1\)

A. \(A\)

\(6x^2+kx+6=0\) is a quadratic equation in which \(k\) is a constant. Its roots \(\alpha\) and \(\beta\) are positive. \(\log_{10} \alpha + \log_{10} \beta = \)

A. \(0\)
B. \(1\)
C. \(\log_{10} 6\)

\(-3x^2-3x = -3(x+a)^2+b\) is an identity in \(x\). What are the values of the constants \(a\) and \(b\)?

A. \(a=1, b=0\)
B. \(a=1/2, b=3/4\)
C. \(a=1/2, b=3/4\)
D. \(a=1/2, b=3/4\)
E. \(a=1/2, b=3/4\)

A. \(a^3b^3c\)
B. \(a^4b^6c^4\)

D. \(a^4b^6c^4\)

A. \(-5\)
B. \(-3\)
C. \(-1\)
D. \(1\)
E. \(10\)

Which of the following can be summed to infinity?
I. The arithmetic progression \(4, 3, 2, 1, \dots\)
II. The geometric progression \(27, 9, 3, 1, \dots\)
III. The geometric progression \(16, -8, 4, -2, \dots\)

A. II only
B. I and II only
C. I and III only
D. II and III only
E. I, II and III

A. \(a:b:c\)
B. \(c:b:a\)
C. \(b+c:a+c:a+b\)
D. \(1/a:1/b:1/c\)
E. \(ab:bc:ca\)

If \(n\) is a positive integer, which of the following numbers is/are odd?

II. \(3(2^n)\)
III. \((2n+1)^2\)

A. II only
B. III only
C. I and III only
D. II and III only
E. I, II and III

A factory employs \(x\) workers each working \(n\) hours a day. The whole factory produces \(k\) watches per day. If \(y\) workers go on leave, then how many hours a day should the remaining workers work in order to produce the same number of watches per day?

A. \(nxy\)
B. \(ny/x\)
C. \(nx/y\)
D. \(nx/(x-y)\)
E. \(n(x-y)/x\)

A. \(8:3\)
B. \(2:1\)
C. \(3:2\)
D. \(4:3\)
E. \(1:1\)
